history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because the commons-fileupload dependency was removed or unmanaged, causing missing package errors.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x no longer transitively includes or manages commons-fileupload in the same way as 2.7. Adding the dependency explicitly via a pom_patch step restores the missing classes on the classpath, allowing the build to proceed to the Java 21 plugin and build upgrades.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because the commons-fileupload dependency was removed from the transitive classpath, causing compilation errors for classes using org.apache.commons.fileupload.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding the commons-fileupload dependency via a POM_PATCH step before the Spring Boot upgrade ensures it is present on the classpath when the UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 recipe runs and compiles the project. This prevents the compilation failure and allows the migration to proceed to Java 21.

history.qwen_observation: The Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step fails compilation because spring-boot-starter-web no longer transitively includes commons-fileupload, causing 'package org.apache.commons.fileupload does not exist' errors.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.2+ replaced commons-fileupload with commons-fileupload2-jdk17 (which uses a different package namespace). Explicitly adding the legacy commons-fileupload:1.5.0 dependency before the Spring Boot upgrade ensures the original imports remain valid on the classpath during and after the migration.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step because the legacy org.apache.commons.fileupload package is missing after Spring Boot 3.x drops the transitive commons-fileupload dependency in favor of commons-fileupload2-jdk17.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x removes the transitive commons-fileupload dependency, causing immediate compilation failures for code importing org.apache.commons.fileupload. Using a POM_PATCH step to explicitly inject commons-fileupload:1.5.0 before the Spring Boot upgrade ensures the artifact is locked on the classpath, bypassing OpenRewrite's dependency management overrides and allowing the SB 3 migration to proceed.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails because Spring Boot 3.3 replaces commons-fileupload with commons-fileupload2-jdk17, changing the package namespace, and prior POM_PATCH attempts to restore the old artifact were ineffective or overridden.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching to OpenRewrite's AddDependency recipe ensures the dependency is correctly injected into the POM's <dependencies> section before the Spring Boot upgrade runs. Placing it first guarantees org.apache.commons.fileupload remains on the classpath and prevents the SB 3.3 meta-recipe from stripping or overriding it during its dependency management updates.
